content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable Gilbert Ferguson 
Member of the Assembly 
state Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Assemblyman Ferguson: 
January 5, 1990 
Re: 
Senate District 31 Election 
Our File No. A-89-706 
We have received your amended Candidate Intention (Form 501) 
and Campaign Bank Account (Form 502) notices in connection with 
the special election for Senate District 31. 
On Form 502, you did 
not provide the number of your campaign bank account. 
Please 
complete the enclosed form as soon as possible to provide the bank 
account number. 
For your information, as both an elected state officer and a 
candidate for Senate District 31, you will incur campaign 
disclosure obligations during 1990 under both sections 84200.7 and 
84200.8 of the political Reform Act. 1 
section 84200.7 requires 
the filing of pre-election campaign statements in connection with 
the June 5, 1990 state primary election. 
section 84200.8 requires 
the filing of pre-election campaign statements in connection with 
the February 6 and April 10, 1990 special and special run-off 
elections. 
However, some of these statements can be combined 
pursuant to section 84205, which states: 
The Commission may by regulation or written advice 
permit candidates and committees to file campaign 
statements combining statements and reports required 
to be filed by this title. 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660 


Honorable Gilbert Ferguson 
Page 2 
Therefore, you should file campaign statements through 
June 30, 1990, using the enclosed schedule. 2 
In addition, please 
refer to the 1989 FPPC "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure 
Provisions of the Political Reform Act" for detailed information 
regarding campaign disclosure. 
(For example, the second pre-
election statement filed in connection witn each election must be 
sent by guaranteed overnight mail or delivered in person.) 
The 
1990 Information Manual will be available in February. 
If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact 
me at (916) 322-5662. 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
{! a~ ('\. CU /{/uLLov.J 
Carla J.(wArdlow 
Assistan~/Chief, Technical 
Assistance & Analysis Division 
The Commission has advised that whenever a candidate or 
officeholder is required to file campaign statements, all of his 
or her controlled committees (other than a committee formed 
primarily to qualify, support or oppose a state ballot measure) 
must file campaign statements at the same times and in the same 
places as the candidate. 
(Barrett Advice Letter, No. A-88-150j88-
168, copy enclosed.) 


Honorable Gilbert Ferguson 
Page 3 
FEBRUARY 6 AND APRIL 10 ELECTION FILING SCHEDULE 
FOR ELECTED STATE OFFICERS AND OFFICEHOLDERS 
WHO WILL ALSO BE VOTED UPON JUNE 5, 1990 
FOR CANDIDATES BEING VOTED UPON ON FEBRUARY 6, 1990: 
TYQe of statement 
Period Covered 
Filing Deadline 
1st Pre-Election 
Jan. 11 -
Dec. 23 
Dec. 28, 1989 
2nd Pre-Election 
Dec. 24 - Jan. 20 
Jan. 25, 1990 
Late Contribution 
Jan. 21 - Feb. 5 
within 24 Hours 
Reporting Period 
FOR CANDIDATES ALSO BEING VOTED UPON IN THE APRIL 10 RUN-OFF: 
1st Pre-Election 
2nd Pre-Election 
Late Contribution 
Reporting Period 
2nd Pre-Election (for 
June election) 
Jan. 21 -
Feb. 24 
Feb. 25 -
March 24 
March 25 - April 9 
March 25 -
May 19 
FOR CANDIDATES WHO LOSE THE FEBRUARY 6 ELECTION: 
1st Pre-Election (for 
June election) 
2nd Pre-Election (for 
June election) 
Late Contribution 
Reporting Period 
FOR ALL CANDIDATES: 
Semi-Annual 
Jan. 21 - March 17 
March 18 -
May 19 
May 20 - June 4 
The day after the 
closing date of the 
last statement filed -
June 30 
March 1, 1990 
March 29, 1990 
Within 24 Hours 
May 24, 1990 
March 22, 1990 
May 24, 1990 
within 24 Hours 
July 31 1 
1990 
1 
The period covered by any campaign statement begins on the 
day after the closing date of the last statement filed or 
January 1 if no previous statement has been filed.

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations and deadlines for an elected state officer who is simultaneously running as a candidate in special elections and a primary election in 1990?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: As both an elected state officer and a candidate for Senate District 31, Assemblyman Ferguson must file campaign disclosure statements under sections 84200.7 and 84200.8 for the February 6, April 10, and June 5, 1990 elections, with statements potentially combinable under section 84205. A detailed filing schedule is provided with specific deadlines for pre-election statements, late contribution reports, and semi-annual filings through June 30, 1990.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document structured as formal advice letter without traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections. Legal analysis embedded in body paragraphs. Includes detailed filing schedules as appendix. Request for missing bank account number information on Form 502.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations and deadlines for an elected state officer who is simultaneously running as a candidate in special elections and a primary election in 1990?

CONCLUSION: As both an elected state officer and a candidate for Senate District 31, Assemblyman Ferguson must file campaign disclosure statements under sections 84200.7 and 84200.8 for the February 6, April 10, and June 5, 1990 elections, with statements potentially combinable under section 84205. A detailed filing schedule is provided with specific deadlines for pre-election statements, late contribution reports, and semi-annual filings through June 30, 1990.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter to Assemblyman Gilbert Ferguson addressing his campaign disclosure obligations for the 1990 Senate District 31 special election and concurrent state primary election, including requirements to file pre-election statements under multiple code sections and a comprehensive filing schedule with deadlines.
